etymology.description: semantic 正(正) (zhèng, "straight") + phonetic 曰(曰) (yuē)
etymology.interpretation: The noonday sun (日) directly overhead is the natural standard of straightness/uprightness (正 "straight"). What is upright is what is correct -> 'right, correct' -> what is correct is what 'is so' -> affirmation 'yes' and the copula 'to be'.

examples.chinese: 他是我的朋友(他是我的朋友)。
这本书是他的(這本書是他的)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū shì tā de.
